name: Polenta
recipeInstructions:
- or yellow cornmeal
1. Bring 6 cups of water to a boil in a 4-quart saucepan. Add salt to taste. Slowly add meal in fine stream, stirring simultaneously with a wooden spoon.
2. Continue to cook over low heat, stirring mixture constantly, for about 1 hour. Additional boiling water, 1 tablespoon at a time, may be added if the mixture becomes too thick to stir.
3. Cook for an additional 3 minutes without stirring, shaking the pot slightly so steam forms under polenta. Pour polenta onto a wooden surface that has been rinsed with cold water, about 18 inches in diameter.
4. Use a spatula to spread polenta into a circular mound. Slice or cut into wedges by sliding a piece of kitchen twine underneath and then drawing up through the polenta.
